nation: Fond du Lac Denesuline First Nation
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Fond du Lac First Nation Development Limited Partnership (99.90%), Fond du Lac First Nation (2008) Development Corporation (100%), Waterfound Development Corp (33.33%), and Waterfound Development Limited Partnership (33.33%). Also holds portfolio investments in CanAlaska Uranium Ltd (400,000 shares, fair market value $250,720), Prince Albert Development Corporation (8.33%), and Prince Albert First Nation Business Development Limited Partnership (8.25%). Member of Prince Albert Grand Council, Athabasca Health Authority, Athabasca Denesuline Education Authority, Athabasca Enterprise Region Corporation, and Athabasca Denesuline Child and Family Services.
notes: Audit is qualified due to missing budget information required under PSAS. Fiscal year is 2022 (year ended March 31, 2022) but auditors report was signed November 7, 2024 - a significant delay of over 2.5 years. Three subsidiaries fully consolidated (Fond du Lac Mineral Resources Inc., Fond du Lac Power Corporation, Fond du Lac Hydro Inc.) and four GBEs on modified equity basis. Deferred revenue increased significantly from $11.2M to $17.9M, primarily driven by ISC capital project funding. Large restricted cash balance of $14.3M for capital projects including WTP expansion and school renovations. The Fond du Lac Specific Claim Trust is excluded from consolidation. OSR appears negative because deferred revenue net adjustment is a large negative component offsetting gross revenues. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + AHA + CMHC + PAGC + PA First Nation Development Corporation + FNT + Northern Lights + Trust funds.
